shell.topbarSubtitle: Configure, test, and operate self-hosted agents

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Review agents, retrieval, tool calls, and runtime protection so you know whether this environment is ready for launch testing.

help.agentReadiness: At least one enabled agent is required before you can test or invoke the runtime.
help.invokeSignal: Number of agent invocations in the last 24 hours. A value of 0 usually means no Playground or external API test has run yet.
help.retrievalSignal: Number of knowledge retrieval attempts in the last 24 hours. After binding a knowledge base, ask a grounded question in Playground to verify this increases.
help.circuitHealth: Circuit breakers protect external services. Healthy means no service is currently blocked because of repeated failures.
help.totalRequests: Total requests entering the agent runtime, including Playground and external API calls.
help.retrieval: RAG retrieval count. It shows whether agents are querying knowledge sources when they need business facts.
help.toolCalls: Number of HTTP or built-in tool calls made by agents. Use it to confirm backend integrations are being used.
help.avgLatency: Average knowledge retrieval latency. If it is high, review top_k, chunk size, vector model, or reranker settings.
help.avgLLMLatency: Average model call latency. Provider speed, context length, and tool rounds all affect this.
help.circuitBreaker: Closed means normal calls; Open means calls are temporarily blocked after repeated failures; Half Open means the system is probing recovery.

agents.title: Agent Management
agents.name: Agent Name
agents.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this agent?

help.capabilitySource: This is the source of truth for what the agent can access at runtime. Shortcut bindings in Integrations write back here; they do not create a second config system.
help.knowledgeCap: Select knowledge sources this agent can search. During chat, the LLM decides when to call the knowledge retrieval tool based on the user's question.
help.workflowCap: Select workflows this agent can start. Workflow names, descriptions, and steps are exposed to the LLM so it can decide when a flow should begin.
help.toolCap: Select external APIs or built-in tools this agent can call. Tool names, descriptions, and parameter schemas are exposed as function calling capabilities.
help.resourceDescription: This is not a separate trigger rule. It is the runtime capability description shown to the LLM. Prefer maintaining the resource's own name, description, and schema.
help.advancedInstruction: Normally leave this empty. Use it only when this specific agent needs extra rules for the bound resource, such as requiring user confirmation first.

workflows.title: Workflow Management
workflows.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this workflow?

knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.description: Upload documents, maintain fast-answer entries, test retrieval, and bind knowledge sources to agent capabilities.
knowledge.supportedFormats: TXT / MD / PDF / DOCX / CSV / XLSX
knowledge.ingestionStandardValue: Text first, table fields preserved, OCR scanned files first
knowledge.question: Question
knowledge.questionPlaceholder: What documents are required for residence registration?
knowledge.autoCreateSource: Leave empty to auto-create from filename
knowledge.uploadStandardDesc: Default single-file limit is 50MB. DOCX extracts headings, paragraphs, and tables before chunking. TXT/MD supports UTF-8, GB18030, and UTF-16. CSV/XLSX should use a header row. PDFs should contain selectable text; OCR scanned files first.
knowledge.emptySources: No knowledge sources yet. Upload a document or create a source first.
knowledge.emptyChunks: This source has no chunks yet
knowledge.deleteConfirm: Delete this knowledge source and all of its chunks?

help.page: When an agent answers a knowledge question, Aezab searches the bound sources first: fast KV, BM25 keyword, and vector retrieval run together, results are fused and optionally reranked, then the LLM answers with that context.
help.sourceType: Choose how the material should be organized: Document for manuals and policies, FAQ for standard Q&A, KV for exact facts like phone numbers or prices, Structured Table for CSV/XLSX rows.
help.domain: A domain is a retrieval namespace. Agents only search sources bound to their capabilities. Use separate domains when business lines should not retrieve each other's content.
help.chunkCount: How many searchable chunks this source contains. Too few chunks can miss facts; too many can increase retrieval cost.
help.ingestionStandard: Use selectable text when possible. DOCX extracts paragraphs and tables; CSV/XLSX should have a header row; scanned PDFs need OCR first.
help.source: Select a source to append the file there, or leave it empty to create a new source from the filename.
help.file: Supported formats: TXT, MD, PDF, DOCX, CSV, XLSX. PDFs should contain selectable text; OCR scanned files first.
help.chunkSize: Approximate characters per chunk. The default 500 works for most support documents; long policy text can use a larger value.
help.chunkOverlap: Characters repeated between adjacent chunks so answers are not cut at boundaries. The default 50 is usually enough.
help.entityKey: The lookup key for fast KV answers, such as support phone or refund window. Exact hits can return stable facts without heavy generation.
help.faq: FAQ stores standard question-answer pairs. User wording does not need to match exactly; retrieval uses similarity.
help.search: This tests retrieval only, not the full agent answer. Use Playground to test the complete runtime path.
